name: Shakti Spear
description: The Shakti Spear, also known as the Vel in Tamil, is a divine spear associated primarily with the Hindu god Kartikeya (also known as Skanda or Murugan), the god of war and victory. It is a symbol of power, courage, and divine energy. The Shakti Spear is not merely a weapon; it represents the concentrated power of the goddess Shakti, the divine feminine energy and the source of all creation. It embodies her strength, wisdom, and unwavering resolve. The origin of the Shakti Spear is often linked to Parvati, the consort of Shiva, who bestowed it upon Kartikeya to vanquish the demon Surapadman. The spear possesses immense power, capable of piercing through illusions and destroying evil forces. It is believed to grant victory in battle and protect its wielder from harm. The spear's power is amplified by Kartikeya's divine status as a son of Shiva and Parvati, making it an unstoppable force against injustice. It is revered as a sacred artifact, representing the triumph of good over evil and the embodiment of divine power. The spear is often depicted as a long, slender weapon, sometimes adorned with intricate carvings and precious stones. Its presence inspires faith and courage in devotees, reminding them of the power of righteousness and the ultimate victory of good over evil. The Shakti Spear continues to be a powerful symbol in Hindu tradition, particularly in South India, where Kartikeya is widely worshipped.
subtitle: Divine spear of courage and victory
material: Divine Energy, possibly metal (gold or celestial iron)
origin: Bestowed upon Kartikeya by Parvati to defeat Surapadman.
currentLocation: Worshipped in temples, often depicted in Kartikeya's hand.

keyMyths.title: Defeat of Surapadman
keyMyths.description: Kartikeya used the Shakti Spear to defeat the demon Surapadman, who had terrorized the gods and the world. The spear pierced through Surapadman's illusions and ultimately vanquished him.

keyMyths.title: Gift from Parvati
keyMyths.description: Parvati, recognizing the need for a powerful weapon to combat evil, infused her divine energy into the creation of the Shakti Spear and presented it to her son, Kartikeya. This act symbolized her blessing and empowerment of Kartikeya's divine mission.

sources.author: G.S. Ghurye
sources.description: Provides context on the symbolism of weapons in Indian culture.
sources.text: Provides context on the symbolism of weapons in Indian culture.

sources.author: Stella Kramrisch
sources.description: Offers insights into the iconography of Shiva and his family, including Kartikeya.
sources.text: Offers insights into the iconography of Shiva and his family, including Kartikeya.

sources.author: Swami Sivananda
sources.description: Devotional work on Kartikeya and the importance of the Vel.
sources.text: Devotional work on Kartikeya and the importance of the Vel.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Like the Shakti Spear, Poseidon's trident is a powerful weapon associated with a major deity, symbolizing dominion and power over a specific domain (the sea). Both are iconic symbols of their respective god's authority and ability to wield immense force.
Gungnir, Odin's spear, shares the Shakti Spear's association with divine authority and war. Both weapons are not just tools of combat, but symbols of leadership and a god's power to command victory.

Jupiter's Thunderbolt represents divine authority and power, similar to the Shakti Spear. While a different form of weapon, both are wielded by powerful gods (Jupiter/Indra/Kartikeya) and are associated with decisive action and dominance.
Lugh's spear is a magical weapon associated with the Celtic god Lugh, a warrior deity. Both spears are symbols of divine power, skill in battle, and the ability to overcome challenges.

companions.name: Kartikeya (Skanda/Murugan)
companions.description: The primary wielder and owner of the Shakti Spear. Kartikeya is the god of war and victory, and the spear is his signature weapon, representing his divine power and authority.

companions.name: Parvati
companions.description: As Kartikeya's mother, Parvati is considered the source of the Shakti Spear's divine energy. She empowered Kartikeya to defeat evil forces using the spear.

companions.name: Devasena
companions.description: One of Kartikeya's consorts, representing divine will and strength. Devasena often accompanies Kartikeya and signifies the divine union of power and wisdom.

companions.name: Idumban
companions.description: A devoted devotee of Kartikeya, often depicted as guarding the hills and temples associated with the god. Idumban's unwavering loyalty and service exemplify the power of devotion to the deity and his spear.

companions.name: His Peacock (Paravani)
companions.description: Kartikeya's vahana (mount), representing victory and spiritual advancement. The peacock carries Kartikeya, enabling him to swiftly travel and utilize the Shakti Spear against evil.
